name: Western Union Money Order Only
description_short: Western Union is a global money transfer service enabling customers to send and receive money internationally with competitive exchange rates and low fees starting at $0.
description_long: Western Union has operated as a trusted money transfer provider for decades, facilitating cross-border financial transactions for millions of consumers worldwide. The company offers online money transfer services with a focus on international remittances, allowing users to send funds from the United States to over 200 countries and territories. Their service includes competitive foreign exchange rates that vary by destination, fee structures ranging from $0 to $1.99 USD depending on promotions and transfer type, and the ability to receive money in multiple currency options. Western Union distinguishes itself through extensive global reach, offering transfers to destinations like Mexico, Philippines, India, Guatemala, Colombia, and virtually every major country worldwide, with specific currency support for each region. The company provides promotional incentives such as $0 fees on first online transfers for registered users and better exchange rates for account holders. While Western Union is a well-established global money transfer service, consumers should note that the website content provided focuses primarily on online transfer functionality rather than traditional money order services. The fee structure and exchange rates are transparent and competitive, though final costs depend on destination country, transfer amount, and current exchange rates.
pros: Offers $0 fee promotions on first international online transfers for registered users
Provides competitive foreign exchange rates with transparency (e.g., 1.00 USD = 16.9723 to 17.6573 MXN)
Global reach to 200+ countries and territories with multi-currency support
Flexible fee structure starting as low as $0 USD up to $1.99 depending on transfer details
Online registration available to access better rates and reduced fees
Real-time exchange rate quotes visible before completing transfer
Supports major remittance corridors (Mexico, Philippines, India, Guatemala, Colombia, etc.)
cons: Website shows service interruptions (Orange wallet temporarily unavailable per error message)
Exchange rates vary significantly by destination and corridor, making comparisons difficult
Fee structure is variable and dependent on multiple factors not fully detailed on homepage
No mention of traditional money order services on the provided website content despite company profile name reference
Limited information about customer support, dispute resolution, or fund delivery timeframes
best_for: International remittance senders transferring funds to family in Mexico, Philippines, India, or Central America
Expatriates and immigrants maintaining financial connections to home countries
Businesses making regular cross-border payments to established destinations
Consumers seeking competitive exchange rates with promotional $0 fee offers
diagnosis: Western Union is best for international remittance senders, particularly those transferring to established corridors like Mexico, the Philippines, and India, who can benefit from promotional $0 fees and competitive exchange rates through account registration. The main caveat is that the website content focuses on online transfers rather than traditional money order services, and users should verify specific fees and exchange rates for their destination country before committing to a transfer.
services: Online international money transfers from United States
Foreign exchange services with real-time rate quotes
Transfers to Mexico with peso (MXN) currency support
Transfers to Philippines with Philippine peso (PHP) and USD options
Transfers to India with Indian rupee (INR) support
Transfers to Guatemala with quetzal (GTQ) support
Transfers to Colombia with Colombian peso (COP) support
User account registration for improved rates
Promotional fee waivers on first registered user transfers
Send amount customization with real-time receiver payout calculation
Support for transfers to US military bases and territories
